hero.title: Reframing Architecture in Palestine
hero.subtitle: A cooperative of architects reshaping design practice through collaboration, innovation, and cultural identity

home.mission: Reframing how architecture is practiced in Palestine.
home.missionSecondary: Reframing architectural practice in Palestine — collectively, in place, and for the future.
home.heroDescription: We are a cooperative of Palestinian architects reshaping design practice through collaboration, cultural identity, and contemporary craft. Our work bridges heritage and innovation.
home.coopDescription: Reframing Architects is a Palestinian architecture cooperative — a collective of creative architects, designers, and thinkers united by a shared belief: architecture is an act of cultural resilience and collective imagination. We work as a cooperative, pooling diverse expertise to deliver design that no single practice could achieve alone.
home.coopDescription2: Our cooperative model ensures that every member has equal voice, fair access to projects, and shared ownership. We don't compete — we collaborate. The result is work that is richer, more thoughtful, and deeply connected to the communities we serve.
home.servicesSubtitle: Four pillars of practice united by a shared commitment to Palestinian architecture and design excellence.
home.featuredProjectsSubtitle: Selected work across residential, cultural, and public spaces.
home.featuredProductsSubtitle: Handcrafted design objects rooted in Palestinian materiality.
home.ourArchitectsSubtitle: A diverse collective of designers, thinkers, and makers.
home.upcomingWorkshopsSubtitle: Monthly educational programmes fostering design literacy and community dialogue.
home.ctaBookDesc: Schedule a consultation with one of our architects to discuss your project, space, or design vision.
home.ctaJoinDesc: Are you an architect or designer? Apply to become a member of our cooperative and shape the future of Palestinian design.
home.ctaNewsletterDesc: Stay connected with our latest projects, workshops, insights, and community updates delivered to your inbox.
home.ctaQuestionDesc: Have a question about our services, pricing, or approach? Reach out — we'd love to hear from you.

about.title: About Us
about.pageSubtitle: A cooperative of Palestinian architects, designers, and thinkers united by a shared belief: that architecture is an act of cultural resilience and collective imagination.
about.vision: We envision a Palestine where architecture serves as a tool for cultural preservation, community empowerment, and sustainable development.
about.mission: To build a cooperative practice that centers Palestinian identity in every design decision — merging heritage with contemporary innovation.
about.background: Reframing Architects was founded as a cooperative of Palestinian architects committed to reshaping how architecture is practiced in our context. We believe design is a collective act rooted in place, memory, and aspiration.
about.values: Collaboration, cultural authenticity, sustainability, community ownership, and design excellence.
about.visionText: A Palestinian built environment that reflects the dignity, creativity, and aspirations of its people — where every structure tells a story of belonging and every space nurtures community life.
about.missionText: To provide accessible, high-quality architectural services through a cooperative model that empowers architects, serves communities, and preserves Palestinian design heritage while embracing contemporary innovation.
about.originSubtitle: From shared frustration to shared purpose.
about.originP1: Reframing Architects was founded by a group of Palestinian architects who recognised that the traditional firm model often sidelines emerging talent and disconnects design from the communities it serves.
about.originP2: In 2023, six architects came together with a radical idea: a cooperative where every member has equal ownership, equal voice, and equal access to meaningful projects. The result is a practice that puts people — both architects and communities — at the centre of every decision.
about.originP3: Our Japandi-influenced aesthetic reflects our dual commitment: the warmth and materiality of Palestinian craft traditions paired with the restraint and clarity of contemporary minimalism.
about.problemText: Palestinian architecture practice faces systemic challenges: limited access to quality projects for emerging architects, fragmented design services, and a growing disconnect between modern construction and cultural identity. Conventional firm hierarchies concentrate opportunity at the top, leaving talented young architects without meaningful work.
about.solutionText: Reframing Architects addresses these gaps through collective ownership. By sharing projects, resources, knowledge, and revenue, we create an ecosystem where architects grow together. Our interdisciplinary approach — spanning architecture, product design, consultations, and education — ensures comprehensive service delivery and sustainable practice.
about.valuesSubtitle: The principles that guide every project, decision, and relationship.
about.collaborationDesc: We work as a collective, pooling diverse expertise to deliver architecture that no single practice could achieve alone.
about.fairOpportunitiesDesc: Every member has an equal voice. We distribute projects, revenue, and recognition equitably across the cooperative.
about.communityImpactDesc: Our work serves the communities we belong to — creating spaces that strengthen social bonds and cultural continuity.
about.innovationDesc: We embrace new materials, technologies, and methodologies while respecting traditional Palestinian building knowledge.
about.partnersSubtitle: Organisations and institutions we work with to amplify impact.

services.title: Our Services
services.pageSubtitle: Four interconnected disciplines — each strengthened by the cooperative's collective expertise. From design to education, we offer a full spectrum of architectural services.
services.architecturalDesignDesc: From concept to completion, we design residential, commercial, and cultural spaces that reflect Palestinian identity while embracing contemporary building practice. Every project begins with deep listening — to the site, the community, and the client.
services.productDesignDesc: Our product line bridges Japandi minimalism with Palestinian materiality — furniture, lighting, and objects crafted from local materials with global design sensibility. Each piece tells a story of place and purpose.
services.consultationsDesc: Expert guidance for individuals, organisations, and municipalities. Whether you need spatial planning advice, material selection support, or a sustainability audit — our consultants bring decades of combined experience.
services.workshopsDesc: Educational programmes that foster design literacy, hands-on craft skills, and community dialogue. Our workshops are open to students, professionals, and anyone curious about architecture and design.
services.pricingDisclaimer: All prices are indicative and may vary based on project scope and requirements. Contact us for a detailed quote.
services.architectLevelDesc: Service fees are adjusted based on the architect's experience level.
services.notSureTitle: Not sure which service you need?
services.notSureDesc: Book a free introductory call and we'll help you find the right approach for your project.
services.fastDeliveryDesc: Need your project completed faster? We offer priority scheduling with multiple architects working in parallel.
services.pricingDisclaimerFull: Pricing varies depending on project scope, architect selection, and level of detail. Final quotes are confirmed after consultation.

collaboratePage.title: Collaborate With Us
collaboratePage.subtitle: Architecture thrives on partnership. We welcome collaborations with institutions, organisations, and fellow design professionals who share our commitment to meaningful, community-centred work.
collaboratePage.howWeCollaborateSubtitle: Multiple pathways for partnership and joint endeavour.
collaboratePage.partnershipsDesc: Partner with us on joint projects, exhibitions, and academic programmes with universities, research centres, and cultural institutions that advance Palestinian architectural discourse.
collaboratePage.fundingDesc: Support cooperative initiatives, sponsor workshops, fund research programmes, and invest in the future of Palestinian design practice and education.
collaboratePage.trainingDesc: Exchange programmes, mentorship opportunities, and skill-sharing between organisations — building bridges across borders through design knowledge.
collaboratePage.projectsDesc: Joint ventures, competitions, and cross-border design projects that bring together diverse perspectives and create architecture of lasting significance.

joinPage.title: Join the Cooperative
joinPage.subtitle: We are always looking for talented Palestinian architects, designers, and thinkers who believe in the power of collective practice. If you share our values, we want to hear from you.
joinPage.communityDesc: Join a supportive network of peers who share knowledge, resources, and creative energy. You are never alone in your practice.
joinPage.fairOpportunitiesDesc: Every member has equal access to projects, revenue, and professional development. No hidden hierarchies.
joinPage.sharedResourcesDesc: Access shared studio space, equipment, software licences, and a collective knowledge base that strengthens your work.
joinPage.professionalGrowthDesc: Mentorship from senior members, funded workshops and conferences, and continuous learning opportunities within the collective.
joinPage.visibilityDesc: Your work is showcased on our platform and across our network, reaching clients, institutions, and collaborators internationally.
joinPage.collaborationDesc: Work on diverse projects across typologies with fellow members, expanding your skills and portfolio far beyond what solo practice allows.
joinPage.whoWeAreLookingForSubtitle: We welcome applications from Palestinian architects, interior designers, urban planners, landscape architects, researchers, and architecture students who are passionate about design and community impact.
joinPage.lookingForArchitectsDesc: Practising architects and designers at any career stage with a portfolio that demonstrates design thinking and cultural sensitivity.
joinPage.lookingForResearchersDesc: Architecture researchers, urban theorists, and academics who can contribute to the cooperative's intellectual and educational programmes.
joinPage.lookingForStudentsDesc: Final-year students and recent graduates seeking mentorship, real-world project experience, and a supportive entry into professional practice.
joinPage.applicationProcessSubtitle: From first contact to full membership — here is how it works.
joinPage.step1Desc: Complete the form below with your portfolio and a statement about why you want to join.
joinPage.step2Desc: Our membership committee reviews your work and assesses alignment with cooperative values.
joinPage.step3Desc: A conversation with current members to discuss your vision, experience, and aspirations.
joinPage.step4Desc: The cooperative votes on membership and you begin contributing to real projects with the team.
joinPage.applyNowSubtitle: Fill out the form below and share your portfolio.

joinPage.errorMessage: There was an error submitting your application. Please try again or email us directly.

joinPage.faqSubtitle: Common questions about joining the cooperative.
joinPage.faq1Question: Do I need to be based in Palestine to join?
joinPage.faq1Answer: While we are rooted in Palestine, we welcome members from the diaspora. Remote collaboration is fully supported, and we regularly work across borders.
joinPage.faq2Answer: There is a small annual membership contribution that covers shared resources and operational costs. This is discussed during the interview stage and is kept accessible for all career levels.
joinPage.faq3Answer: Yes. Final-year architecture students and recent graduates are encouraged to apply. We offer mentorship tracks and guided project participation.
joinPage.faq4Question: How long does the application process take?
joinPage.faq4Answer: Typically 2-4 weeks from submission to final decision. We aim to be thorough but respectful of your time.
